texto: Your mind is like Heraclitus' river. Your mind, in fact, is like nineteenth-century father of psychology William James' "stream of consciousness," a bubbling, babbling brook. Your mind constantly produces different currents of associations, different swirls of thought, and different moods.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Howard Bloom: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Your mind is like Heraclitus' river. Your mind, in fact, is like nineteenth-century father of psy…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, science, different pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: Your mind is like Heraclitus' river. Your mind, in fact, is like nineteenth-century father of psychology William James' "stream of consciousness," a bubbling, babbling brook. Your mind constantly produces different currents of associations, different swirls of thought, and different moods.
